cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A systematic, objective method for measuring psychological characteristics or behaviors

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The consistency of a test — it produces the same results across repeated measurements

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The extent to which a test measures what it is designed to measure

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The extent to which a test's content represents all the material it is designed to measure

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: How well a test predicts future performance or behavior

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Standardization scores based on a large representative sample; what a score means relative to the group

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Ensuring all test-takers receive the same questions, conditions, and scoring procedures

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The capacity to learn from experience, solve problems, and adapt to new situations

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A score representing a person's intellectual ability relative to age peers (average = 100)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Alfred Binet
cards.answer: Developed first practical intelligence test to identify children needing educational support; introduced concept of mental age

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Lewis Terman
cards.answer: Adapted Binet's test for American use (Stanford-Binet); coined the term 'intelligence quotient' (IQ)

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: David Wechsler
cards.answer: Created the WAIS and WISC intelligence tests measuring verbal and nonverbal abilities separately

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Spearman's concept of a general underlying intelligence factor that contributes to all intellectual tasks

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Charles Spearman
cards.answer: Proposed the concept of general intelligence (g) through factor analysis of cognitive test scores

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Howard Gardner
cards.answer: Proposed theory of multiple intelligences: linguistic, logical-mathematical, spatial, musical, bodily-kinesthetic, interpersonal, intrapersonal, naturalist

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Robert Sternberg
cards.answer: Proposed triarchic theory of intelligence: analytical, creative, and practical intelligence

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The intellectual ability to reason speedily, think flexibly, and solve novel problems; peaks in early adulthood

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The accumulated knowledge and verbal skills gained through experience; maintained or grows into old age

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The ability to perceive, understand, manage, and use emotions effectively; proposed by Goleman

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The generation-over-generation rise in IQ scores observed worldwide in the 20th century

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: IQ of 70 or below combined with significant deficits in adaptive functioning; formerly called mental retardation

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: IQ of 130 or above, often combined with exceptional talent or creativity

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: The tendency to perform worse on tests when aware of a negative stereotype about one's group

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Achievement tests that measure what a person has already learned in a specific area

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Tests designed to predict future performance based on current abilities and potential

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: A normal distribution on a graph showing the bell-shaped frequency of scores around the mean

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Test questions that unfairly advantage members of particular cultural or social groups

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Tests designed to assess general ability independent of specific cultural experience

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Widely-administered college admissions test; was originally called Scholastic Aptitude Test

cards.subject: ap-psychology
cards.prompt: Heritability of IQ is approximately 50%, yet IQ has risen dramatically with better nutrition and education — this illustrates
